description:
# ExampleCommando

ExampleCommando is a PowerNukkitX example plugin demonstrating how to use the `commando` library. It shows how to create commands with argument parsing, optional/required arguments, and execution constraints.

## Features

- Register multiple commands from a single plugin.
- Optional and required arguments with `RawStringArgument` and `BooleanArgument`.
- In-game only constraint with `InGameRequiredConstraint`.
- Console only constraint with `ConsoleRequiredConstraint`.
- Fat JAR build with Maven Shade to include `commando` dependency.

## Commands

| Command | Audience | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `/greet [name]` | All | Greet someone by name. | `/greet Steve` |
| `/warp <location>` | In-game | Command with required argument and in-game constraint. | `/warp spawn` |
| `/echo` | All | Simple echo command with no arguments. | `/echo` |
| `/serverinfo` | Console | Show online players and TPS. | `/serverinfo` |
| `/hidden [true\|false]` | In-game | Toggle hidden mode with boolean argument. | `/hidden true` |

## Structure

```text
src/main/java/com/bofoiii/examplecommando/
  ExampleCommando.java      # Plugin entry point
  GreetCommand.java         # Optional RawStringArgument
  WarpCommand.java          # Required RawStringArgument + in-game constraint
  EchoCommand.java          # Command without arguments
  ServerInfoCommand.java    # Console-only constraint
  HiddenCommand.java        # Optional BooleanArgument
src/main/resources/plugin.yml
```

## Build

Make sure the `commando` artifact is installed in your local Maven repository, then run:

```bash
mvn package
```

Output JAR:

```text
target/ExampleCommando.jar
```

## Install

Copy the built JAR to your PowerNukkitX server's `plugins/` folder:

```text
plugins/ExampleCommando.jar
```

Restart the server and check the log:

```text
ExampleCommando enabled!
```

## Notes

`dependency-reduced-pom.xml` is auto-generated by Maven Shade and can be ignored.
